type: CLAIM

type: DEFER
files: Tests/test_image.py
spec: Please add tests for dry_run in Tests/test_image.py. Spec for dry_run feature: Image.save(fp, format=None, dry_run=False, **params) - a new keyword-only-ish param 'dry_run' (default False) on Image.save(). When dry_run=True: the method still resolves format/filename, calls _ensure_mutable(), sets up encoderinfo/encoderconfig, selects save_handler (SAVE or SAVE_ALL), and INVOKES the save_handler against a throwaway in-memory buffer (io.BytesIO()) instead of opening/writing the real file at 'fp' -- so any exceptions the encoder would raise (e.g. unsupported mode for format) still raise normally. No file is created/opened/removed on disk when dry_run=True, and the real fp is never touched. Returns None as usual. Suggested tests: 1) save(tmp_path/'out.png', dry_run=True) then assert the file does NOT exist at that path. 2) dry_run=True with a normal save afterwards (dry_run=False) still produces a valid file with correct content, to show dry_run didn't corrupt any state (encoderinfo cleaned up in finally). 3) dry_run=True still raises for an incompatible mode/format combo (e.g. saving CMYK to a format that doesn't support it, or similar existing error case) -- reuse an existing 'unsupported mode' test scenario if one exists in the file. 4) dry_run=True works when fp is a filename string, not just file object. Place tests near existing test_save / test_fp tests in Tests/test_image.py.

type: CLAIM
files: src/PIL/Image.py
note: I will own Image.py and implement both backup/backup_suffix (mine) and dry_run (yours) in save(). Please send SPEC for dry_run.

type: DEFER
files: Tests/test_image.py
spec: Please own Tests/test_image.py and add tests for backup feature: (1) test_save_backup - create a temp file, save an image to it once to create it, then call img.save(path, backup=True) and assert a file at path+'.bak' exists and its content equals the pre-save original bytes (read original bytes before the second save). (2) test_save_backup_suffix - same but pass backup_suffix='.backup' and assert the backup file is created at path+'.backup' instead of path+'.bak'. (3) test_save_backup_no_existing_file - saving to a brand-new (non-existent) path with backup=True should NOT create any backup file and should not raise. (4) test_save_backup_false_default - saving without backup param (default False) should not create a .bak file. Use tempfile / helper_get_dpi-style temp file helpers already used elsewhere in Tests/test_image.py (see existing save tests for patterns, e.g. tmp_path fixture).
note: backup, backup_suffix params on Image.save()

Spec for dry_run feature: Image.save(fp, format=None, dry_run=False, **params) - a new keyword param 'dry_run' (default False) on Image.save(). When dry_run=True: the method still resolves format/filename, calls _ensure_mutable(), sets up encoderinfo/encoderconfig, selects save_handler (SAVE or SAVE_ALL), and INVOKES the save_handler against a throwaway in-memory buffer (io.BytesIO()) instead of opening/writing the real file at 'fp' -- so any exceptions the encoder would raise (e.g. unsupported mode for format) still raise normally. No file is created/opened/removed on disk when dry_run=True, and the real fp is never touched/opened. Returns None as usual.

Please reply with a single CLAIM for src/PIL/Image.py to confirm and we'll both proceed to implementation -- no further reassignment after that.

type: DONE
files: src/PIL/Image.py
note: Implemented backup/backup_suffix and dry_run in Image.save(). Signature: save(self, fp, format=None, *, dry_run=False, backup=False, backup_suffix='.bak', **params). dry_run runs save_handler against io.BytesIO(), no disk I/O, exceptions propagate. backup copies existing file to filename+backup_suffix via shutil.copy2 before opening for write, only when file already exists (skipped for newly-created files). All existing Tests/test_image.py tests still pass (192 passed). Ready for your test additions.

type: DONE
files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Note: our environments are separate — your edits to Image.py never appeared in my copy (file mtime unchanged), so I implemented dry_run myself in my own copy and dropped the backup tests from my patch (no backup impl present here). My patch.txt is self-contained for dry_run only. You should make sure your own patch.txt independently contains your backup implementation + tests, since cross-environment file sharing isn't actually happening despite the coordination messages.
